review_text: Lovely location pleasant office staff

Campers that hav animals ,  should not have them on site , and then go out , leaving them behind . And I don't think people can have a go about the council putting up the price ... If some campers didn't spend half an hour in the showers , which happens often , then perhaps improvement could be made ... Quick showers like you would do in your own house , not wasting water .. that might keep the prices down .

We just finished our first ever camping and motorhome experience at Warrnambool Surfside Caravan Park as part of the Homeschool in Camp and it was honestly amazing.

I didn’t expect to enjoy it as much as I did. We usually stay in five star hotels so I was a bit hesitant at first but this completely exceeded my expectations. It turned out to be such a special and memorable experience for our family.

The staff were all incredibly friendly and welcoming which made everything feel easy from the start. The campgrounds are clean well maintained and in the best location. We were right by the beach and could hear the ocean at night and I honestly have not slept that well in a long time.

The facilities are clean and well looked after and everything you need is close by. It is about a ten minute walk into town with great cafes restaurants and bars. There is an IGA nearby a beautiful park right across the road for the kids and the Surfside Lifesaving Club just around the corner. Flagstaff Hill is also within walking distance.

Warrnambool is stunning and Surfside Caravan Park is the perfect place to experience it all. We absolutely loved our stay and will definitely be back.

Sehr großer Campingplatz mit vielen Plätzen. Es liegt sehr schön direkt entlang des Strandes. Die WC-Dusch-Gebäude sind etwas in die Jahre gekommen, aber sauber. Aufgrund der Größe trifft man unterschiedliche Menschengruppen. Wie auch in anderen Rezensionen beschrieben wurde, wurde die Ruhe mehrfach durch andere Gäste gestört, die sich nicht an die üblichen Camping-Regeln gehalten haben. Hier wäre eine konsequentere Durchsetzung der Platzordnung wünschenswert.
Did not stay here as no cabins available for my booking, however staff very helpful to assist with access to join the rest of our group for daily activies.

Great caravan park, beach very close and clean when we were there over the Australia day weekend. Only thing that could make the park better is if it had a pool option. Very family friendly, and close walking distance to mini golf and the local park for the kids to play.

Another stay at this holiday park. It’s our absolute favourite! This holiday park is very clean, the trucks come every day during summer to empty the bins (the kids enjoy watching this) The shower / toilet blocks get cleaned a couple times a day. The kids enjoyed the scavenger hunt which gave them time to explore each day. It’s just such a nice place to stay. Love love love it!
Love it here, been coming regularly last 40 years.  Beach is great, shopping is good plenty to do. Caravan park well kept

You come to this caravan park because the camp site is close to the beach; just over the sanddune. The council yearly put up the cost of the sites, for what improvements, you say, no improvement to ammenities, very poor salary increases to to the staff and beach clean up. Well this year, the beach was disgusting. Normally the camper could swim at the beach just over the sand dunes. The lack of beach dressing for the campers would drown any swimmer entering the water for a paddle.
All each camper can do is pay the massive pay rise next year or leave this beloved place and not come.
Do the council care about the people who pay so much each year, for a very mediocre park that could be be so much better.
The beach was disgusting this year!

The staff were extremely friendly knowledgeable while remaining professional. They accommodated my change in circumstances without hesitation.
Highly recommended and I will be returning most definitely 🙂

completely over priced because of school holidays. WIFI does NOT work at all. Unsafest I've felt in a campsite and we've crossed the Nullabour. NOISY. Next to an active train line. NO Enforcement of dog leash rules. The worst. Drunk men loitering outside the ladies toilets at night. can't wait to leave
Great caravan park, very clean facilities great camp kitchens, plenty of bbqs
Close to town. Across the road is a awesome playground. Clean amenities and plenty of them. Fabulous camp kitchen.
Would go back there again

Nice clean park and well-kept amenities. You couldn’t really hear the ocean from our site, but we saw wild animals around and even a little bunny, which was a fun surprise for Ted! Overall a peaceful stop close to everything we needed. 🐇🌾

整體環境乾淨整潔
就是浴室水小了點
Camped great amenities- camp kitchen is the best camp kitchen I’ve come across - stoves - kettles toaster and an oven!!! Sites are a little small well the one we stayed on was.
Great location next to the beach

Camping here in a Saturday night is very very noisy with cars doing burn outs and engine heads speeding up and down the road all night & I mean all night!
It was terrible.

The site itself is immaculate! The showers & camp kitchen is perfect.
The staff were friendly & helpful.

The rooms here were great. Spacious, clean and had everything we could need. The only drawback was a noisy air conditioner right above the main bed. Cold without it running, hard to sleep with the noise when it was.
Great location and really friendly service.
Nice park, good amenities close to cbd👍
Perfect position...enjoyed the proximity to the beach as well as the town.

This was our 2nd stay. Stayed in one of the chalets and had excellent nights sleep. Bed was wonderfully comfy. Facilities in the rooms were great. Cooked up a lovely meat and veg meal in the kitchen. When booking the staff were professional and helpful.
Nice and peacefull in the winter, facilities were A+

Great staff, it was my first time camping in Australia and I wasn't sure of a few things but they sorted me out. I arrived by bicycle and set my tent up no worries.

They did advise that there had been cases of stolen bicycles and offered to lock it up inside the office. Some people in the reviews had said that was a concern.

Kitchen was great, and bathrooms were clean too. The heater in the dining area didn't work though, so you might want to look into that.

Great location and again very friendly front desk. Thank you!

We had a great time at Surfside, it was my first time staying at a campsite. We stayed on a powered site during Easter weekend. The staff were friendly and helpful, it was super easy to get hooked up to the power. The facilities were nice and clean and the showers were nice and hot and right in front of the beach. The price was also very reasonable at $80 a night, I will definitely be staying Again.

The staff and the facilities were amazing, great access to all the beaches Justin walking distance through the back of the caravan park
And a big thank you to a staff there

Great big campsite, booked unpowered and we had the whole grass patch of sites to ourselves. Great shower facility, best pressured tap I’ve had at a campground. Great staff, very friendly and welcoming. Would definitely return

I am a regular visitor at Surfside, have been for a few years. I have always experienced the utmost courtesy and friendliness from ALL of the staff here, including Manager, Office, Cleaners, and Grounds staff. I will keep coming back!

Amazing caravan park, very large, LOTS of amenities. Pricing is great for powered site.
Service from staff is amazing, they managed to fit us in on very short notice with very polite and easy going attitude.
Close to the beach & parks, really good if you have kids!
Very close to hot springs and other things in area.
There’s also a little takeaway at reception.

Our family of 3 stayed in a Cedar cabin.|Location of the park is amazing, a 2 min walk to the swimming beach, lake pertobe and Beach kiosk cafe. This was the highlight for us. |The staff were lovely as well- our son unfortunately had a vomit on the first night and they kindly cleaned and changed our linen in the cabin the next day. |The cabins are a bit worn out, could do with some refurbishment. Our shower made loud noises and was very temperamental (going from hot to cold erratically), the flyscreen door didn't slide well, mattress on main bed was too soft (springs worn out).|Overall, it was value for money given its prime location and is ok for a few nights' stay.
Great campsite to take a break from driving the great ocean road. Very friendly staff, beautiful location, and really nice facilities, we were happy to pay $55 for our 1-night stay

Wir sind durch Zufall Anfang Dezember an dem Campingplatz vorbeigekommen und haben kurzfristig eine Nacht dort gebucht. Das Personal war sehr nett und zuvorkommend. Wir hatten ein guten Stellplatz, von wo aus alles gut und schnell erreichbar war. Wir (2 Erw. + 2 Kinder) haben uns hier sehr wohl gefühlt.
Really nice holiday park. Return visit for sure.

My  two daughters only went on one school camp.They saved their own money to go on their first holiday  with their aunty.Both My daughters were disappointed  worse than Faulty  Towers drama. My youngest daughter  burnt her head and  nearly 2 months later her  scalp is still red and sore.My daughters also told me that there aunty had issues in the bathroom.when my daughters came home they said they wouldn't  go back.l  even rang the Warnambool  council the lady was lovely  telling me that there were issues including slack of staff.she  apologised  for the bad experience  that My sister and daughter had
However l was told that someone would text from surfside.It doesn't  matter weather it was cheaper or even in the school holidays,you need to run a business  for tourists to come back to your town.That is what brings in money  for your community.

Stayed here a few years back ... Very friendly staff helpful.grouds kept neat ..kitchen area got everything you
need just one of the best parks I have stayed in ... Will be staying again soon

I can in seeking an unpowered site and having  autism they didn't have any that would allow me time to recharge so they moved me to a powered location but kept the fees reduced so I could have a quiet stay and even happily welcomed my service dog and kitty I can't recommend this place enough they're very disability focused and helped me to the absolute best of thier capabilities I will definitely be returning

I have been coming to this park for a number of years and I can’t see us returning any time soon.
The sites were dust and no maintenance was carried out during our time there.
If you are going to charge $49.50 per night for a powered site I believe it should have some grass and some amenities you have nothing, no pool or anything for entertainment.
In summary to expensive for a second rate park..🤬🤬
We didn't stay here but the staff here helped us out while we were passing through town and customer service is top notch. Location looked really great as well.

Didn't have 1 issue at all.
Great place to stay as a family.
We took our camper and stayed for 5 nights.

We stayed for 3 nights, the Staff Veronica and Taylor were amazing and went above and beyond in their role to make our stay great.  This was very much appreciated and our site was perfect.  The only downside to our stay was the person or persons that decided it was ok to steal small items off our table in our annexe.  To say we were annoyed is an understatement.  If they had needed something just ask and would have tried to help them but don't be a petty thief.
But I guess you can have bottom feeding scum like this anywhere, a good thing we had the big items secured with lockdown cables.  This shouldn't be necessary but now I am pleased we take that precaution everywhere

Amazing!!! This is the third time|We have stayed at this caravan park. This time we got a different site. Was right across from a toilet / laundry block which was great. Shower pressure is great which is good to wash sand off. The park it self if very clean and tidy. The ladies in the office were very helpful and it’s great that the kiosk is open during the summer holidays. Will be back thats for sure!!

We have been coming here for years to camp, we stay at the breakwater end of the park. This end only gets opened up at peak times like Xmas and Easter. The amenities are very old and dated but kept clean and tidy. The sites have bushes and hedges to help with the wind and a bit of privacy. The shop at the front has good take way food. There is lots to see and do. The beach is just over the sand dunes. There is a massive kids playground and and bbq area on the other side.
very good Holiday park great location near the surf life saving club and the beach

The park is well located with great proximity to the beach. Very well organised regarding pin code park access. Accommodating for our urgent need to park up early. Unfortunately, we timed it when a Schoolies group were celebrating the end of year. Loud music after 10pm and absolutely no campsite etiquette, walking through our campsite at 10:30pm, talking loudly. We accepted the train sounds but unfortunately the loud noise from car exhaust and motorbikes was more frequent than expected. Also, assuming for safety, the lights on the toilet facilities are very bright, and with a hot night and windows open, the lights shone through into our caravan.
Excellent  location on foreshore . Comfortable  spacious  cabin

If you are travelling with kids it is straight opposite the kids park which is large and has heaps of equipment. The holiday park doesn't have anything for kids. It has a lot of rabbits, which would be in the hundreds of them, so loads and loads of rabbit droppings which could not be avoided which isn't plesent for the kids to play around. We also had multiple issues (3 in total) with our boom gate code not working which we were lucky was all in the office hours so after a quick trip to see the office they reset it and fixed it up.

On our second night, around 9 pm, we got locked out at the boom gate entrance because our passcode wasn’t working, along with several other guests experiencing the same issue.

Taking the initiative, I located their after hours emergency contact number and called for assistance. However, the person I spoke with was not only unhelpful but also offensive and dismissive throughout the conversation. Rather than offering assistance, he continually accused me of letting other people into the campground using the same passcode. To be clear, I had not met any of the other drivers, each of whom had their own individual barcode numbers. His accusation was baseless and offensive.

Furthermore, he went so far as to question whether I even had a booking at the site, insinuating that I was attempting to sneak in. This was not only insulting but completely illogical, as my caravan was already parked inside the campground.

You would expect a popular campground such as this to have reliable systems in place and courteous, professional staff, especially for after-hours emergencies.

This is a well located Caravan park in Warrnambool. Council run. It's really big with well maintained amenities.  Right on the beach, with easy access for a walk along the clean sands. Bring a jumper as the wind can be bracing. All good 👍.  Don't forget to check out the adjacent Flagstaff Hill historical area and see the night-time presentation 😀 👍.  There is an adjacent train line, with a few trains each day, but not too much of a distraction
Great place for camping 🏕

Park is in a great location, right on the other side of sand dunes is a walking track and lovely long stretch of beach. Reception staff were friendly, however I can't understand why we were put so far away from everyone else and amenities when the park is quite empty and we'reonly here one night. It's really the boondocks out here, no shade, rabbit holes absolutely everywhere and a long walk just to go to toilet. Yet there were plenty of powered sites (what we paid for) available that were in better position, had shade and concrete pads.  Amenities need updating, grotty and run down for what you pay for.
Fantastic caravan park, close to the main street, great views, ideal location.
Great cabins and great location
Great showers v clean everywhere close to beach and very close to one of the best fish and chip shops.

Called late in the afternoon and given a fabulous site by the Beach.
Facilities very clean and tidy.
Would come back.

Very helpful team in the Office
Well cared for park & amenities
BBQ's, camp kitchen with ovens, him tops & work benches (only kettles & toasters provided. No other cooking utensils, pots pans etc provided)
Visited in September school holiday period.  Dogs allowed!
Watch out for the rabbits
A very large caravan park right on the beach. Great that u can bring dogs. Cheap washing machines $3/load. LOTS of rabbits & rabbit holes. Mostly grassy sites with a few slabs. Good camp kitchen.

There weren’t too many people staying when we arrived but they decided to put everyone together rather than space everyone out?! Site is a bit run down and the most ridiculous part was no plates, cutlery and frankly anything in the kitchen area! (See photos) Had to bring our own crockery to the kitchen to cook. Worth staying at the BIG4 in town instead.

This caravan park would have to be the best in Warrnambool, beach access , close to all the parks and recreational activities |Clean tidy with extremely friendly staff, we chose to stay another night because there was so much to do just around the park itself

We had a great time! The highlight was the kindness of the staff at the front desk. We had an almost DV situation and how swiftly the staff acted was top notch!! They ensured our utmost safety and then comfort. They followed up with us everyday to make sure we were ok. Words fail me

Great place to stay, is just walk way to the beach. Nice view and pathway. Big holiday park with great amenities and a botanic garden just walk a few minutes. Good for kids to explore. The place was relaxing.

Cabins are tired looking. WiFi is not very good. Need new mattress in cabins was terrible to sleep on turned it over but didn’t help much. TV reception in the bedroom was woeful. Good position from town centre and meal venues. Staff on reception were ok
Very well run. Incredible location
Beautiful staff and a beautiful place to stay stay

Not to stay at hotel.. just a meal and play in the park with the kidss..

park was so wide and comfortable
has many things to do.. restauran near by.. all is nice for a short escape

We stayed in this park in our caravan up the top section good size site close to a amenities block which was older but clean. Walkways throughout the park takes you onto the beach and shops would be about 20 min walk.The thing i noticed was there is no play ground for families in the park but outside across the road is the biggest and best parks ive ever seen for kids.

Epic location. Very short walk to the beach. Walk through a tunnel and your at a wooden maze, playground and lakes. The caravan park was massive. All camp and caravan sites seemed decent sized. We stayed in a 2 room cabin provided by Camp Quality. It had all the amenities needed holiday but if you don't want to do dishes there's also a cool looking camp kitchen with bbqs and seating undercover. Heaps of shower/toilet blocks so there wouldn't be a line if you camped. Plenty of restaurants in walking distance too. Didn't need to really see the staff besides when we checked in our out but were very welcoming.I'd definitely stay here again

Feel like sardines in here. Powered sites are ok for size but they are extremely close together and during holiday season it’s extremely overcrowded. The bathrooms are decent and clean but the sizes of the cubicles are on the smaller side. Staff at the kiosk are quite rude and seem arrogant and miserable. The site is right next to the beach however no dogs are allowed past the entry point during a certain period (there are signs to inform you of this) the huge park is right across the road and Flagstaff Marinetime Village is right next door. Overall it’s a decent place to camp but just very overcrowded and tight.
Awesome place to camp! A little busy but am awesome location to stay for a night

Really nice holiday park with access to the beach and walking tracks.
Great surf and access to the kids playground and pool.

We stopped in last minute on the Thursday before this long weekend and the staff were lovely.
They were so accommodating, quick to help and really welcoming.
We pulled up after dark and we could hear the waves but we couldn’t see them, when we woke up we couldn’t believe how close to the beautiful beach we were! It was beautiful!
We didn’t have our children with us this time but definitely will next visit as the park is a perfect place to stop with kids.
Thank you for one of the best weekends we have ever had!

This is a fantastic park, it is right on the foreshore, well laid out and with a great walking path down to the breakwater. The park area is so really great and the whole area is wonderfully maintained. Our cabin was reasonably clean, quite roomy and with all the facilities we needed. We'd come back.

Theft at the park is rampant, just short of sleeping with your possessions they most likely will be stolen. Reception doesn't inform you and the council who run it take no responsibility to prevent it. It is a lovely caravan park and Warrnambool  is awesome.
Stay somewhere else if you can't bolt things down.

We booked site with our dog we now know afterpaying that can't take our dog on beach until April and we can not the him into the park across the road so will not be going anywhere and we can't get refund and bloody rabbits every where. Go to hopkins caravan park cheaper allows dogs have river for them.

Rampant theft from park. Council run park but little is done to either inform visitors of the theft issues. Security over Christmas Period but once Security ceased rampant theft re started. Ebikes stolen almost nightly or attempted theft. Don't leave anything out that you want in the morning. We had our ebikes chained but they were still stolen. Maybe on check in staff show warn visitors in person!. We had spoken to several other people staying in the park who all said that they only knew from other visitors and not the park management. Be very careful staying at this park!

This was a last minute camping trip with the kids. We booked on arrival and the lot we were given had beach access literally 5 steps away from our camp which was perfect. Lady in the office was very helpful on arrival. The toilet block we used was very clean and the showers worked great. Nice clean holiday park with lots of little rabbits and possums running around for the kids to watch. Will defiantly come back!

Zuerst das positive: die Anlage ist sehr sauber und das Check In war freundlich und unkompliziert! |Wir fühlten uns wie auf einem Parklatz auf einem Supermarkt eingepfercht zwischen den vielen Autos. Ich denke so könnten sich Tier in der Massentierhaltung fühlen. Mit Abstand der teuerste und überfüllteste  Campingplatz auf unserer ganzen Australienreise. Gott sei dank haben wir nur für eine Nacht gebucht. Wifi funktioniert auch nicht.
Excellent facilities,very clean, great camp kitchen, friendly staff
Great place to relax, well worth the visit to warrnambool

Went there just after New year's Eve and it was definitely the biggest and busiest holiday park I have been too. Saying that even with all the people, facilities were still pretty clean. And you have pretty much direct access to the beach.

A big caravan park heaps of space and great environment. Amenities at the park was great and central to access easily. The location of the park is right next to the beach and on a road that's close to other business for leisure and relaxation. Staff at front desk was very nice and moved sites for us so we could camp next to our camp mates.
Der Preis ist mit 79 AUD für eine "powered site" sehr grenzwertig. Unser Platz war direkt an der Hauptstrasse, so weit weg, dass man keinen WLAN-Empfang mehr hatte.
Tip tip eingerichtet. Viele Hasen auf dem Camping. Alles sehr sauber. Normalgrosser Standplatz. Nicht auszusetzen

I took a campground for puttting 2 tent
It’s was around 70$
The common kitchen have nothing, just fridge and gas cooker
Over priced no pets allowed over Christmas period

The whole camp was infested with rabbits, lots of droppings everywhere. Otherwise great location right next to the beach and Flagstaff Maritime village+ modern & clean facilities. No toilet brushes, so toilets were quite disgusting. Their cancellation policy is a bit strict over the holiday season: no refund within 30 days of arrival ... Boom gate code also applies when you exit the campsite.
It's nice for travelling if you need a place to stop, I just really don't like these industrial style massive caravan parks.

Stayed in the caravan park with our caravan, good size site off season, great location near surfbeach, access path within park and walking trails, if fit you could walk into town. Must do is Fletcher Jones vintage market and a walk around the maritime museum. Warrnambool pub a must for a meal, caught an uber from caravan park cost around $11 on a Saturday.

We recently stayed here with my family. We rented a Beach Chalet. Accomodation was great, clean, well equiped (although I would suggest adding a mop + bucket). It is very close to the beach and to an amazing playground; great location. The kids loved the park and we will definitely return!! Lovely customer service and easy to deal with. If you have children or a caravan/tent or just want a lovely cosy park near the beach - I highly recommend it!
Best place for kids
Good park. Nice and quiet.
Brilliant, clean and laid back park, great walks, friendly people, plenty of room.

Great location - right on the beach and clean facilities. Camp kitchen is well equipped with BBQ’s undercover.
The park is massive! I can imagine how wonderful for family’s it would be during peak times 👍

Alles sehr gut, es gibt Duschen, Toiletten, Abspülmöglichkeiten, eine riesige Küche mit Toastern, etc. Grauwasser- und Toilettenentleerung. Die Duschen und Toiletten waren sehr sauber, der Park wirkt insgesamt sehr gepflegt.

In der Nacht kann man Hasen sehen, die direkt bis zum Camper kommen.
Excellent facilities,  outstanding service at reception including knowledgeable about surrounding activities and facilities.  Beautiful spot between beach and Flagstaff Hill maritime village! 10*

2 night minimum stay off season mid week.
Lady didn't even look up from her computer to answer.

Arrived in the dark for our first night's booking and were impressed with the process at short notice and with the facilities and amenities open during the winter months Very clean and well equipped. Unfortunately we were not aware we are next to a train line and on top of that on a Friday night it is 1:00 a.m. as I write this post and we have been unable to sleep because it seems we are next to a raceway as the locals continually drive there loud vehicles in circuits close to the park... it has been non-stop for four hours so far!!!

Fantastic park, great services and friendly staff.
Cabins were great, including fridge, kettle toaster, shower and all linens and towels. We had a fantastic stay.
A great little spot for a family getaway. A great park across the road
Fantastic service and really liked the kids play area. Fantastic for our little one.
